varieties.name: Lycium barbarum (Common Red Goji)
varieties.traits: Deciduous shrub, 1-3m tall, sprawling habit, spiny branches, grey-green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, up to 3200 kg/acre at maturity
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent adaptation to Brisbane subtropical climate. Heat tolerant to 38°C, handles humidity well. Can be grown year-round in Brisbane. Drought tolerant once established. Brisbane growers report good success in warm humid conditions. Self-fertile. Purple flowers May-September, fruit July-October.
varieties.availability: Widely available Queensland nurseries, Daley's Fruit Nursery, The Seed Collection, Mudbrick Herb Cottage

varieties.name: Crimson Star (Ningxia #1, Ningqi No. 1)
varieties.origin: Northern China, Ningxia Province commercial variety
varieties.traits: 6-8 ft tall, vigorous grower, productive variety
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding, begins bearing 1-2 years after planting

varieties.name: Phoenix Tears
varieties.origin: West Utah selection from Asian stock
varieties.traits: 5-6 ft tall, 5 ft wide, multi-stemmed, vigorous
varieties.yield_notes: Extended harvest (4 weeks), fruits adhere well to stems
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent subtropical adaptation for Brisbane. Sweet flavor with slight acidity. Very drought tolerant. Hardy to -15°F. Fruits ripen over long period mid-summer to frost. Good for humid Brisbane climate.
varieties.availability: Available through specialty nurseries, Stark Bros., One Green World

varieties.name: Sweet Lifeberry
varieties.origin: Selected strain from China, proprietary European development
varieties.traits: Sprawling shrub, 5-7 ft, flexible canes, grey-green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Continues flowering and fruiting through first heavy frost
varieties.growing_notes: Heat tolerant to AHS zone 9, excellent for Brisbane subtropical climate. Very tolerant of hot and dry conditions. Self-fruitful. Hardy to USDA zone 5. Royal purple flowers late spring/early summer. Grows sweeter as matures on plant.

varieties.name: Big Lifeberry
varieties.origin: Selected strain from China, European variety
varieties.traits: Similar to Sweet Lifeberry, sprawling habit, 5-7 ft
varieties.growing_notes: Heat tolerant, excellent for Brisbane subtropical conditions. Hardy to zone 5, heat tolerant to zone 9. Self-fertile. Tolerates hot dry climates well, adapts to humidity.

varieties.name: Golden Goji (Golden variety)
varieties.traits: Similar growth to red varieties, 5-7 ft
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane subtropical climate. No bitterness, delicate flesh, no smell. Higher flavonoid content (60% more than red). Sweeter taste preferred by many. Heat and humidity tolerant. Self-fertile.

varieties.name: Golden Jewel
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for Brisbane. Heat tolerant. Similar qualities to Golden Goji.

varieties.name: Firecracker
varieties.traits: Self-fertile, highly productive, similar growth to other goji

varieties.name: Red Empress
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for Brisbane subtropical climate. Heat and drought tolerant once established.

varieties.name: Lycium chinense (Chinese Wolfberry)
varieties.origin: China, traditional cultivation thousands of years
varieties.traits: More vine-like than L. barbarum, larger oblong leaves, sprawling
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent subtropical adaptation for Brisbane. More vine-like growth requires support/trellis. Good for humid climates. Leaves also edible (cooked). Hardy and drought tolerant once established. Can tolerate Brisbane summer heat and humidity.
varieties.availability: Available Queensland nurseries, Mudbrick Herb Cottage, online seed suppliers

varieties.name: Ningqi No. 7
varieties.growing_notes: Good adaptation potential for Brisbane subtropical climate. Bred for stress resistance - beneficial for variable subtropical weather. Rich nutrition profile.

varieties.name: Ningqi No. 9
varieties.traits: Fast growth, strong stress resistance, optimized variety

varieties.name: Black Goji (Lycium ruthenicum)
varieties.origin: Qinghai-Tibet Plateau, Himalayan Mountains, Central Asia
varieties.traits: 4-6 ft tall, shrub form, thorny branches, light gray-green fleshy leaves

varieties.name: Lycium pallidum (Pale Wolfberry)
varieties.origin: Northern Mexico, Southwestern United States, native species
varieties.traits: 1-3m tall, dense thorny branches, pale leaves, thicket-forming
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent heat and drought tolerance for Brisbane. Native to desert regions, very resilient. Fragrant creamy-yellow/greenish flowers. Can spread via cuttings and suckering. Historic use by Native Americans - dried and ground into meal. Adapts to various soils.
varieties.availability: Specialty native plant nurseries, seed suppliers

varieties.name: Lycium europaeum (European Tea-tree)
varieties.origin: Mediterranean region - Portugal, Spain, France, parts of Asia and North Africa
varieties.growing_notes: Can adapt to Brisbane subtropical climate. Young shoots cooked as vegetable. Rich in vitamins A, C, E, flavonoids. Flowers June-August. Tolerates maritime exposure - good for coastal Brisbane. Good nutritional profile similar to other Lycium species.

varieties.name: Ningqicai-1 (Leaf Vegetable Variety)
varieties.traits: Bred for leaf production, larger oblong leaves

varieties.name: Queensland Subtropical Adapted Seedlings
varieties.traits: Variable growth patterns, naturally selected for local conditions
varieties.growing_notes: Seedlings from plants grown successfully in Brisbane area show good local adaptation. Genetic diversity provides resilience. Many Brisbane gardeners report success with seedling-grown plants that adapt well to humid subtropical conditions. Variable quality but often surprisingly productive.
varieties.availability: Local Brisbane garden centers, seed saving networks, Subtropical Fruit Club members

varieties.name: Lycium barbarum (Common Red Goji)
varieties.traits: Deciduous shrub, 1-3m tall, may be semi-evergreen in tropical Kerala climate
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to high in Kerala conditions
varieties.growing_notes: Adaptable to Kerala tropical monsoon climate. Prefers 15-30°C temperature range - Kerala suits this well. Can handle monsoon humidity. Requires good drainage to prevent root rot during heavy rains. May fruit year-round in Kerala. Self-fertile. Known as "wolfberry" in India.
varieties.availability: Available in India - IndiaMART suppliers, green nursery Bangalore, Amazon India, veliyath gardens

varieties.name: Lycium chinense (Chinese Wolfberry)
varieties.traits: More vine-like, larger oblong leaves, sprawling habit
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent tropical monsoon adaptation for Kerala. Handles high humidity well. Vine habit suits Kerala trellis growing. Leaves cooked as vegetable (common in Asia). More heat-tolerant than L. barbarum. Good for year-round production in Kerala.
varieties.availability: Available India, seed suppliers, online nurseries

varieties.name: Kashmir Himalayan Lycium
varieties.origin: Kashmir Himalayas, native to India-China-Tibet borders
varieties.traits: Traditional wild and cultivated types, variable growth
varieties.yield_notes: Variable depending on elevation and conditions
varieties.growing_notes: Indigenous to Indian Himalayan regions. Cultivated in Kashmir, Jammu region. JKMPIC (Jammu Kashmir Medicinal Plants Centre) developing these for Indian conditions. Grown at higher elevations but can adapt to Kerala with proper care. Traditional use in Indian medicine.
varieties.availability: JKMPIC Jammu, Kashmir agricultural centers, specialty Indian nurseries

varieties.name: Phoenix Tears
varieties.growing_notes: Can adapt to Kerala tropical conditions. Drought tolerant - good between monsoon periods. Heat tolerant. Extended fruiting period beneficial for Kerala. May produce year-round in tropical climate.

varieties.name: Golden Goji
varieties.growing_notes: Good for Kerala tropical climate. Higher flavonoid content. Sweet taste popular in Indian market. Heat and humidity tolerant. Self-fertile.

varieties.name: Sweet Lifeberry
varieties.growing_notes: Adapts to Kerala tropical climate. Very heat tolerant. Can handle humidity with good air circulation. May fruit year-round in Kerala. Self-fruitful.

varieties.name: Big Lifeberry
varieties.growing_notes: Heat tolerant, suitable for Kerala. Self-fertile. Adapts to hot humid conditions. Larger fruits attractive for fresh market.

varieties.name: Firecracker
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for Kerala. Early fruiting beneficial for tropical climate. Heat tolerant. May produce multiple flushes in Kerala climate.

varieties.name: Black Goji (Lycium ruthenicum)
varieties.growing_notes: Can adapt to Kerala with proper drainage. Native to arid regions but surprisingly adaptable. Requires excellent drainage during Kerala monsoons. Unique purple color valuable. Very high antioxidant content. Rare in India.

varieties.name: Ningqi No. 7
varieties.growing_notes: Good potential for Kerala. Stress resistance beneficial for monsoon climate variations. Can handle temperature and moisture fluctuations. Rich nutrition.

varieties.name: Ningqicai-1 (Leaf Vegetable Variety)
varieties.traits: Bred for leaf production, larger leaves
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Kerala. Dual-purpose - leaves as vegetable fit Indian cuisine traditions. Continuous harvest possible. Adapts to tropical monsoon climate. Leaves used fresh or dried for tea.

varieties.name: Tibetan Lycium Varieties
varieties.origin: Tibet and Mongolia traditional growing regions
varieties.traits: Variable, traditionally wildcrafted, adapted to high altitude

varieties.name: Lycium europaeum (European Tea-tree)
varieties.origin: Mediterranean, also found in India (parts of North India)
varieties.growing_notes: Present in India, can adapt to Kerala. Young shoots edible. Used in folk medicine. Rich nutritional profile. Can tolerate varied conditions. Found in some Indian regions, indicating adaptation potential.
varieties.availability: May be found in North Indian nurseries, Mediterranean imports

varieties.name: Indian Tropical Adapted Seedlings
varieties.origin: Open-pollinated from plants adapted to Indian conditions
varieties.traits: Variable, naturally selected for tropical monsoon climate
varieties.growing_notes: Seedlings from plants successfully grown in Kerala and other Indian tropical regions show local adaptation. Genetic diversity provides resilience to monsoon conditions. Plants growing in India (Kashmir, other regions) being propagated and adapted. Kerala farmers experimenting with cultivation report variable but often positive results.
varieties.availability: Local Kerala nurseries, Indian seed networks, JKMPIC propagation programs

varieties.name: Lycium pallidum (Pale Wolfberry)
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent heat tolerance potentially suits Kerala. Desert native shows drought adaptation. Would need testing in Kerala monsoon conditions. Requires excellent drainage. Historic food use documented.
